Regulatory Authority, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–44, title III, § 333131 Stat. 955Aug. 2, 2017“(a).—In GeneralThe President shall, not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], promulgate regulations as necessary for the implementation of this title [see Short Title of 2017 Amendment note set out under ] and the amendments made by this title.Aug. 2, 2017section 9201 of this title“(b).—Notification to CongressNot fewer than 10 days before the promulgation of a regulation under subsection (a), the President shall notify and provide to the appropriate congressional committees [see Definitions note below] the proposed regulation, specifying the provisions of this title or the amendments made by this title that the regulation is implementing.”

Rule of Construction, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–44, title III, § 332131 Stat. 954Aug. 2, 2017“Nothing in this title [see Short Title of 2017 Amendment note set out under ] shall be construed to limit—section 9201 of this title“(1)the authority or obligation of the President to apply the sanctions described in section 104 of the North Korea Sanctions and Policy Enhancement Act of 2016 (), as amended by section 311 of this Act, with regard to persons who meet the criteria for designation under such section, or in any other provision of law; or22 U.S.C. 9214“(2)the authorities of the President pursuant to the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.).”50 U.S.C. 1701

Authority To Consolidate Reports, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–44, title III, § 331131 Stat. 954Aug. 2, 2017“Any reports required to be submitted to the appropriate congressional committees [see Definitions note below] under this title [see Short Title of 2017 Amendment note set out under ] or any amendment made by this title that are subject to deadlines for submission consisting of similar units of time may be consolidated into a single report that is submitted to appropriate congressional committees pursuant to the earlier of such deadlines. The consolidated reports must contain all information required under this title or any amendment made by this title, in addition to all other elements mandated by previous law.”section 9201 of this title

Definitions, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–44, title III, § 302(b)131 Stat. 941Aug. 2, 2017“In this title [see Short Title of 2017 Amendment note set out under ]:section 9201 of this title“(1).—Applicable united nations security council resolution; luxury goodsThe terms ‘applicable United Nations Security Council resolution’ and ‘luxury goods’ have the meanings given those terms, respectively, in section 3 of the North Korea Sanctions and Policy Enhancement Act of 2016 (), as amended by subsection (a).22 U.S.C. 9202“(2).—Appropriate congressional committees; government of north korea; united states personThe terms ‘appropriate congressional committees’, ‘Government of North Korea’, and ‘United States person’ have the meanings given those terms, respectively, in section 3 of the North Korea Sanctions and Policy Enhancement Act of 2016 ().22 U.S.C. 9202“(3).—Foreign person; north korean personThe terms ‘foreign person’ and ‘North Korean person’ have the meanings given those terms, respectively, in paragraph (5) and paragraph (13) of section 3 of the North Korea Sanctions and Policy Enhancement Act of 2016 ( and 9202(13)), as added by subsection (a).22 U.S.C. 9202(5)“(4).—Prohibited weapons programThe term ‘prohibited weapons program’ means—“(A)any program related to the development of nuclear, chemical, or biological weapons, and their means of delivery, including ballistic missiles; and“(B)any program to develop related materials with respect to a program described in subparagraph (A).”

children.label: i
children.text: authorizes the imposition of sanctions on persons for conduct with respect to North Korea;

children.label: ii
children.text: prohibits transactions or activities involving the Government of North Korea; or

children.label: iii
children.text: otherwise imposes sanctions with respect to North Korea; and

children.label: i
children.text: authorizes the imposition of sanctions on persons for conduct with respect to North Korea;

children.label: ii
children.text: prohibits transactions or activities involving the Government of North Korea; or

children.label: iii
children.text: otherwise imposes sanctions with respect to North Korea.

children.heading: Applicable United Nations Security Council resolution
children.label: 2 Applicable United Nations Security Council resolution
i
children.text: authorizes the imposition of sanctions on persons for conduct with respect to North Korea;

children.label: ii
children.text: prohibits transactions or activities involving the Government of North Korea; or

children.label: iii
children.text: otherwise imposes sanctions with respect to North Korea.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs, the Committee on Financial Services, and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Designated person
children.label: 4 Designated person
children.text: The term “designated person” means a person designated under subsection (a), (b), or (g) of for purposes of applying 1 or more of the sanctions described in subchapter I or II with respect to the person.section 9214 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: an individual who is not a United States citizen or an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence to the United States; or

children.label: B
children.text: an entity that is not a United States person.

children.heading: Government of North Korea
children.label: 6 Government of North Korea
children.text: The term “Government of North Korea” means the Government of North Korea and its agencies, instrumentalities, and controlled entities.

children.heading: Humanitarian assistance
children.label: 7 Humanitarian assistance
children.text: The term “humanitarian assistance” means assistance to meet humanitarian needs, including needs for food, medicine, medical supplies, clothing, and shelter.

children.heading: Intelligence community
children.label: 8 Intelligence community
children.text: The term “intelligence community” has the meaning given such term in .section 3003(4) of title 50

children.label: A
children.text: has the meaning given such term in section 746.4(b)(1) of title 15, Code of Federal Regulations;

children.label: B
children.text: includes the items listed in Supplement No. 1 to part 746 of such title, and any similar items; and

children.label: C
children.text: also includes any items so designated under an applicable United Nations Security Council resolution.

children.heading: Monetary instruments
children.label: 10 Monetary instruments
children.text: The term “monetary instruments” has the meaning given such term in .section 5312(a) of title 31

children.heading: North Korea
children.label: 11 North Korea
children.text: The term “North Korea” means the Democratic People’s Republic of Korea.

children.label: A
children.text: is organized under the laws of North Korea or any jurisdiction within North Korea (including a foreign branch of such an institution);

children.label: B
children.text: is located in North Korea, except for a financial institution that is excluded by the President in accordance with ;section 9228(c) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: is owned or controlled by the Government of North Korea, regardless of location; or

children.label: D
children.text: is owned or controlled by a financial institution described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C), regardless of location.

children.label: A
children.text: a North Korean citizen or national; or

children.label: B
children.text: an entity owned or controlled by the Government of North Korea or by a North Korean citizen or national.

children.label: i
children.text: deny access to or degrade, disrupt, or destroy an information and communications technology system or network; or

children.label: ii
children.text: exfiltrate information from such a system or network without authorization;

children.label: B
children.text: significant destructive malware attacks;

children.label: C
children.text: significant denial of service activities; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other significant activities described in regulations promulgated to implement .section 9214 of this title

children.heading: South Korea
children.label: 15 South Korea
children.text: The term “South Korea” means the Republic of Korea.

children.label: A
children.text: a United States citizen or an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence to the United States; or

children.label: B
children.text: an entity organized under the laws of the United States or of any jurisdiction within the United States, including a foreign branch of such an entity.
